design_philosophy.core_loop: Drive down procedurally-varied roads, destroy enemy vehicles with weapons, avoid collisions and road hazards, earn points to unlock extra lives
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing offense (weapon acquisition/usage) against defensive survival (staying on road, avoiding collisions)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Operate a sentient spy car with gadget-laden weaponry, channeling James Bond vehicular fantasy in arcade action
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentionally arcade-driven with high-speed gameplay, score-based progression tied to lives economy, and punishment systems (score counters freeze on civilian collisions); rejects narrative-heavy delivery in favor of pure mechanical challenge

difficulty.lives_system: Customizable at title screen (0-12 lives selectable via hidden menu); extra lives earned through score thresholds and hidden Hyper Tennis minigame (20 bonus lives for CPU victory)
difficulty.continues_system: Continue/End screen present; optional Hyper Tennis Pong minigame accessible at continue screen for additional lives
difficulty.checkpointing: None; infinite scroll with no stage checkpoints; progression through regional territory forks
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players select starting lives at title screen, control weapon acquisition via weapons van summoning, choose between low/high gears affecting speed/handling tradeoff

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary weapon loadout system: machine gun (infinite), oil slicks, smoke screens, surface-to-air missiles (limited ammo). Weapons reset on car destruction. Speedboat transformation provides alternate weapon configuration (flamethrower replaces oil slick)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Regional territories unlock via periodic road forks; weapons van appears in each new territory; boat transformation triggers via boathouse entry (both optional and forced encounters)
replayability_hooks: Score-based progression creates incentive for high-score runs
Variable road fork choices determine which territories player encounters
Hidden Hyper Tennis minigame provides alternative route to extra lives
Customizable starting lives enable difficulty scaling for replay
No defined ending creates endless progression loop

world_building_techniques: Terrain variety (freeway, different weather/regions via forks)
Enemy vehicle archetypes (helicopters, boats, ground vehicles) establish implicit spy-thriller setting
Weapons van and special gadgets evoke James Bond spy-gadget fantasy
Speedboat transformation suggests mission variety (land/water operations)
Score counter mechanic implies agency evaluation (penalty for civilian harm reinforces spy restraint ethics)

faction_indicators: Civilian vs. enemy vehicle distinction creates implicit ally/threat identification
danger_signals: Enemy vehicles attempting to force player off road
Craters blasted into road by aerial bombardment
environmental_storytelling: Regional territory transitions (different terrain, weather) signal operational scope
Weapons van appearances establish tech supply infrastructure
Boathouse transitions imply dual-environment mission capability
Road forks present meaningful navigation choices without explicit briefing

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Super Spy Hunter article)
wikipedia (Spy Hunter - original arcade game context and James Bond inspiration)
